title: Rollback Trigger And Recovery Plan
description: A release-and-rollout control workflow that manages promotion waves, rollback readiness, and deployment dependencies across environments. Focus case: rollback trigger and recovery plan.
assumptions: Artifacts and configuration snapshots required for rollback are published and immutable at release time.
Runbooks and rollback templates are authored, versioned, and accessible to operators referenced by the workflow.
Approver and operator roles are available and can be invoked within the rollout windows.
Dependencies between components and infrastructure are declared prior to scheduling.
risks: Missing or outdated rollback templates could prevent automated recovery and force manual intervention.
Approval delays or unavailable operators may stall critical rollback or recovery actions during incidents.
Incorrect dependency reservation can create partial rollouts that are difficult to revert consistently.
Stale configuration snapshots may cause rollbacks that do not restore a functional system state.
